metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify (or add a scope step for) whether `idea` already exists as a recognized status value in the system's status vocabulary / state machine, and if not, add the file that defines allowed statuses to the Scope and a corresponding AC.
findings.finding: The plan never specifies whether `idea` is already a recognized status value in the system's status vocabulary (enum, allowed-values list, or state-machine definition), nor does it include a scope step to register it if it is new. If the reducer or any validation layer rejects unknown status strings, every AC that exercises `idea` status — including the smoke-test transitions `transition idea open` and `transition idea closed` — will fail without a described fix.
findings.location: Scope section; AC lines for 'ticket has status idea', 'single CREATE event', smoke-test AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet to Scope: 'Confirm `idea` is listed in the status enum / allowed-values registry (e.g. `src/rebar/reducer/_statuses.py` or equivalent); add it if absent.' Add a corresponding AC: 'The status `idea` is present in the system's status vocabulary so that status-validation and transition guards accept it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` to the Scope and extend `_VALID_STATUSES` (and the usage string) to include `"idea"` so that `transition <id> idea open` and `transition <id> idea closed` are accepted as valid `current_status` values.
findings.finding: `transition idea open` (required by the smoke-test AC) will be rejected by the existing `_validate_status` guard because `"idea"` is not in `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked")` in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:29`. The plan's Scope list omits `transition.py`, so this break will not be caught during implementation.
findings.location: Scope section / AC: 'transition idea open promotes it to claimable work'
findings.suggested_fix: In `transition.py`, change `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked")` to `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked", "idea")` and update the usage string. Add `transition.py` to the plan's Scope list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` to the Scope section and extend the `ticket_status` enum to include `'idea'`.
findings.finding: `common.schema.json` defines `ticket_status` as an enum `['open', 'in_progress', 'blocked', 'closed', 'archived', 'deleted']`. The `idea` status is absent. Any schema validator (e.g. `test_schema_coverage.py`, MCP output validation, export schema) applied to an `idea`-status ticket will fail. `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` is not listed in the plan's Scope section.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json:9
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"idea"` to the `ticket_status` enum array in `common.schema.json`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `_VALID_STATUSES` in `transition.py` and the `ticket_status` enum in `common.schema.json` are updated to include `idea`, and add these files to the plan's Scope section — otherwise `transition idea open` (AC13 smoke test) will be rejected by `_validate_status` with a hardcoded 'must be one of' error.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `transition idea open` (and `transition idea closed`) will work after this change, but the codebase's `_validate_status` in `transition.py` rejects any `current_status` not in `_VALID_STATUSES = ('open', 'in_progress', 'closed', 'blocked')` — `idea` is absent. Additionally, `common.schema.json#ticket_status` is a closed enum of 6 values with no `idea`. Neither `transition.py` nor `common.schema.json` appear in the plan's Scope section, leaving the capability assertion in AC13 ('transition idea open promotes it to claimable work') unresolved.
findings.location: Scope section (no mention of transition.py or common.schema.json); AC line: 'transition idea open promotes it to claimable work; transition idea closed succeeds with no completion-gate error'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` — extend `_VALID_STATUSES` to include `"idea"` and update the `_validate_status` error message. Add `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` — extend the `ticket_status` enum to include `"idea"`. Note that `idea` as a `target_status` should probably be disallowed (you can only enter `idea` via CREATE, not via transition), so `_validate_status` may need separate `current_status` and `target_status` allowlists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (add `"idea"` to the `ticket_status` enum) and the `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` regeneration step to the Scope, so that `types.py` stays in sync with the new status and the CI drift-gate passes.
findings.finding: The `ticket_status` enum in `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json` (the authoritative schema from which `src/rebar/types.py` is auto-generated via `gen_types.py` with a CI drift-gate) currently lists only `["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted"]` — `idea` is absent. The plan's Scope does not name `common.schema.json` or `gen_types.py` as edit targets, meaning the generated `TicketStatus` Literal in `types.py` will not include `idea` and the CI drift-gate will fail if `types.py` is edited manually.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json line 8-10 / src/rebar/types.py line 21
findings.suggested_fix: In `common.schema.json`, change `"enum": ["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted"]` to `"enum": ["open", "in_progress", "blocked", "closed", "archived", "deleted", "idea"]`, then run `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types` to regenerate `types.py`. Add both as Scope items.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend `common.schema.json#/$defs/ticket_status` to include `"idea"` and document it as the genesis-only status value created by the `idea` command.
findings.finding: The canonical ticket_status enum in common.schema.json (the vocabulary every consumer relies on) does not include 'idea', yet the plan introduces 'idea' as a live status value without specifying that this enum must be extended.
findings.location: src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json — $defs/ticket_status enum; plan section 'What' / 'Scope'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"idea"` to the `enum` array in `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json#/$defs/ticket_status` and add the new value to the Scope list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `idea` to `_VALID_STATUSES` in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` (and any parallel status-validation list in `txn.py`) so that `transition idea open` and `transition idea closed` succeed as the smoke-test AC requires.
findings.finding: The plan's smoke-test AC ('transition idea open' / 'transition idea closed') requires `idea` to be a valid source status in `transition_compute`, but `_VALID_STATUSES = ('open', 'in_progress', 'closed', 'blocked')` in `transition.py:29` does not include `idea`. The plan never mentions updating `_VALID_STATUSES` or the `_validate_status` guard, so the two smoke-test transitions in AC-13 would both fail with 'invalid current_status' errors.
findings.location: AC line: 'transition idea open promotes it to claimable work; transition idea closed succeeds with no completion-gate error' / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:29
findings.suggested_fix: In `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`, change `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked")` to `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked", "idea")`. Update the usage string and any error message that enumerates valid statuses. Also verify `txn.transition_core` does not independently reject `idea` as a `current_status`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` to the Scope section and extend `_VALID_STATUSES` (and its error message) to include `'idea'`.
findings.finding: The AC requires `rebar transition <id> idea open` and `rebar transition <id> idea closed` to work, but `transition.py` defines `_VALID_STATUSES = ('open', 'in_progress', 'closed', 'blocked')` and `_validate_status()` rejects any value not in that tuple with an error. The plan's Scope section does not list `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section / AC lines 'transition idea open' and 'transition idea closed' / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:29
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'idea'` to `_VALID_STATUSES` in transition.py and update the error-message string: `'Must be one of: open, in_progress, closed, blocked, idea'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` and `src/rebar/_cli/help/idea.txt` to the Scope section, covering the dispatch arm (add `'idea'` to `_WRITES_FULL` or a new intercept) and the pinned help-text file.
findings.finding: The CLI dispatcher in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` routes subcommands via named frozensets and `_dispatch()`. `idea` is absent from `_WRITES_FULL` (and all other sets), there is no dispatch arm for it, and there is no `src/rebar/_cli/help/idea.txt` package-data file. Without these, `rebar idea` will hit the 'Error: unknown subcommand' branch at line 449. Neither `_cli/__init__.py` nor `help/idea.txt` are listed in the plan's Scope section.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py:65-80, 270-274, 449 / src/rebar/_cli/help/
findings.suggested_fix: Add `'idea'` to `_WRITES_FULL` in `_cli/__init__.py` and ensure `commands_main` routes it to the new `idea.py` command; create `src/rebar/_cli/help/idea.txt` with the usage text.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` to Scope and update `_VALID_STATUSES` (and its `_USAGE` string) to include `idea` so that `transition idea open` and `transition idea closed` are accepted by `_validate_status()`, satisfying AC13.
findings.finding: AC13 requires `rebar transition <id> idea open` and `rebar transition <id> idea closed` to succeed, but `_validate_status()` in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` hardcodes `_VALID_STATUSES = ('open', 'in_progress', 'closed', 'blocked')` and will reject `idea` as an invalid current_status with an error. The plan's Scope does not list `transition.py` as a file to edit.
findings.location: AC13 (smoke test) / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py line 29, _validate_status()
findings.suggested_fix: In `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py`, change `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked")` to `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked", "idea")` and update the `_USAGE` string accordingly. Also add `transition.py` to the Scope section of the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `'idea'` to `_VALID_STATUSES` in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py` (and its usage string) so `transition idea open` is accepted, and add `transition.py` to the plan's Scope list.
findings.finding: The transition validation guard `_VALID_STATUSES` in `transition.py` does not include `'idea'`, yet the smoke-test AC requires `transition idea open` to succeed — the plan never mentions updating this decision rule.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:29 — `_VALID_STATUSES`; AC smoke-test item 'transition idea open promotes it to claimable work'
findings.suggested_fix: Update `_VALID_STATUSES = ("open", "in_progress", "closed", "blocked", "idea")` in `transition.py` and update the `_USAGE` string accordingly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `composer.py` (`src/rebar/_commands/composer.py`) to the scope list and specify how `create_core` will forward an optional `status` parameter into the CREATE event data dict so `process_create`'s new `data.get('status', 'open')` path is reachable without duplicating validation logic in `idea.py`.
findings.finding: `composer.py` (`create_core`) is absent from the plan's scope list, yet `create_core` currently does not accept or forward a `status` parameter into the CREATE event data dict. If `idea.py` reuses `create_core` for its validation + alias logic (the established pattern for every other command), `create_core` must also be modified to accept and pass `status` through — otherwise `process_create`'s new `data.get('status', 'open')` path is unreachable via the normal compose path.
findings.location: Scope section — `src/rebar/_commands/idea.py` and `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` listed; `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` absent
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py` to the Scope section with the note: '`create_core` gains an optional `status` keyword argument (default `"open"`); when non-None it is written into the CREATE event data dict alongside the other fields, so `process_create` can read it.' This keeps the single compose path intact and avoids NIH duplication.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` to the Scope section with a pass-2e branch handling `status == 'idea'` (e.g. via `transition_compute(local, 'open', 'idea', cascade=False, repo_root=rr)` after `transition.py` is updated).
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` pass-2e status handler explicitly covers `in_progress`, `blocked`, `archived`, and `closed` but has no branch for `idea`. A round-trip export+import of an `idea`-status ticket will silently leave it in `open` status. `import_ndjson.py` is not listed in the plan's Scope section.
findings.location: Scope section / src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py:279-300

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the AC (or 'Why' section) that the file_impact warning lives in `create_cli` only and will not fire for `idea_cli` calls that go through `create_core` directly — confirm that no active suppression code is needed, or explain the scenario in which it would fire.
findings.finding: The plan's AC item 'The create-time file_impact stderr warning does not fire for a ticket created by rebar idea' implies work is needed to suppress the warning, but the warning is emitted only inside `create_cli` (composer.py lines 268–276), not in `create_core`. Any `idea_cli` that calls `create_core` directly — the pattern every other command follows — will never emit the warning regardless of ticket type. Calling this out as a suppression requirement and including it as an AC item creates phantom work and could mislead the implementer into adding suppression logic or an exempt-types entry where none is needed.
findings.location: AC: 'The create-time file_impact stderr warning does not fire for a ticket created by rebar idea'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan whether `idea.py` calls `create_core` directly (making the warning suppression a no-op) or goes through `create_cli` (requiring `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` or a `genesis_status` parameter to be extended); the current spec is silent on this call path.
findings.finding: The plan says 'Suppress the create-time file_impact stderr warning for idea-created epics' and AC-10 asserts 'The create-time file_impact stderr warning does not fire for a ticket created by rebar idea.' However, the warning lives exclusively in `create_cli` (composer.py:268-277), which is the CLI dispatcher route — NOT in `create_core`. The new `idea.py` command will call `create_core` directly (as the library facade does), so the warning would not fire anyway without any extra suppression work. The plan treats this as a non-trivial scoping concern but the mechanism may be a no-op. Conversely, if `idea.py` does call `create_cli` (or is routed through it), the warning would fire unconditionally for `epic` types unless explicitly suppressed. The plan does not specify which call path `idea.py` uses.
findings.location: Plan section 'Suppress the create-time file_impact stderr warning' / src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:268-277

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan how `idea.py` avoids the file_impact warning: either (a) call `create_core` directly (bypassing `create_cli`'s warning block) and document that as the intent, or (b) add `'epic'` to `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` with a comment scoping it to idea-genesis epics.
findings.finding: The file_impact warning suppression mechanism is under-specified: the existing warning in `create_cli` (composer.py:268) fires based solely on ticket_type, with no status parameter; the plan says to suppress it for idea-created epics but does not specify how the new `idea` command will invoke or bypass this code path.
findings.location: src/rebar/_commands/composer.py:263-276 — file_impact warning; plan AC 'The create-time file_impact stderr warning does not fire for a ticket created by rebar idea'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one test file path to the Scope section (e.g. `tests/test_idea.py` or equivalent) covering the event-log inspection (AC5) and the end-to-end smoke test (AC13).
findings.finding: The Scope section lists no test files, yet AC5 explicitly requires a test that 'inspects the event log', and AC13 explicitly describes an 'end-to-end smoke test' as a deliverable — both are uncovered work in the decomposition.
findings.location: Scope section; AC5; AC13
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/test_idea.py` (or the project's conventional test path) to the Scope section, noting it covers: (a) single-CREATE-event assertion for CLI/MCP/library surfaces, and (b) the end-to-end flow: idea creation → `transition idea open` → `transition idea closed` with no completion-gate error.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or explicit test note) for `process_create` receiving a CREATE event with an invalid/disallowed status value — specify whether the reducer raises, coerces to `open`, or rejects, and add a test for that path.
findings.finding: The new `process_create` behavior of reading an optional `status` field from CREATE event data has no failure-path AC: if the event carries an unrecognized or disallowed status value (e.g., `status: 'invalid_state'` or `status: 'closed'`), the plan specifies no test for what the reducer should do — silently ignore it, raise, or coerce — leaving a silent-corruption path untested.
findings.location: AC section / `src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` — `process_create` optional status
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: '`process_create` raises (or coerces to `open`) when the CREATE event carries a `status` value that is not in the allowed genesis set; a test asserts the invalid-input behavior explicitly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the 'idea is the sole non-`open` genesis status producer' policy and its race-condition rationale in a durable artifact (ADR, CLAUDE.md architecture note, or a dedicated section in `docs/event-schema.md`) so future contributors know this constraint is intentional.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a deliberate architectural constraint — only the `idea` command (never a general `create --status` flag) may produce a non-`open` genesis status — but this policy is not recorded in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or any design doc. Future contributors could unknowingly violate it.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope / Acceptance Criteria item 9
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short section to `docs/event-schema.md` (or a new `docs/adr/NNN-genesis-status-constraint.md`) explaining: (1) why genesis status defaults to `open`; (2) why only the `idea` command may override it; (3) why a general `create --status` flag is prohibited. Reference this from the CLAUDE.md or architecture overview.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define an idempotency strategy for `create_idea` (e.g., caller-supplied `idempotency_key` field, or server-side deduplication on title+timestamp window) so that an MCP client retry does not silently create a duplicate epic.
findings.finding: The MCP `create_idea` tool has no idempotency mechanism, so an at-least-once MCP client retry (e.g., on timeout or transport error) will silently create a duplicate epic — two tickets with the same title in status `idea` — with no way for the caller to detect or deduplicate.
findings.location: `src/rebar/_mcp_writes.py` — `create_idea` MCP write tool; AC: 'The MCP `create_idea` tool creates an `epic` in status `idea` in one CREATE event.'
findings.suggested_fix: Accept an optional `idempotency_key` (UUID) in the `create_idea` request payload; store it in the CREATE event; reject (return the existing ticket ID) if a CREATE event with the same key already exists in the event log. Add a corresponding AC: 'Calling `create_idea` twice with the same `idempotency_key` returns the original ticket ID without creating a second epic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC items (or a dedicated non-happy-path section) specifying: (a) CLI behavior and exit code when title is missing/empty; (b) MCP `create_idea` error response shape on invalid input or backend failure; (c) library `rebar.idea(...)` exception type or result contract on failure.
findings.finding: The plan specifies only the happy path for all three user-facing surfaces (CLI, MCP, library); it does not address input validation or error states — e.g., what happens when the title is empty or omitted, when the storage backend fails during the single CREATE event, what exit code the CLI emits on failure, what the MCP tool returns on error, or what exception/result contract the library exposes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all AC items) and 'What' section describing CLI/MCP/library surfaces
findings.suggested_fix: Add three AC items: (1) '`rebar idea` with no title prints usage error to stderr and exits non-zero'; (2) 'MCP `create_idea` returns a structured error response (not a crash) when title is absent or creation fails'; (3) 'Library `rebar.idea(title="")` raises `ValueError` (or returns a typed error result) on invalid input'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC specifying the behavior (validation error / rejection) when `rebar idea`, `create_idea`, or `rebar.idea` receives an empty or whitespace-only title, and ensure a test covers this path.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion covers an empty or whitespace-only title passed to `rebar idea`, `create_idea`, or `rebar.idea(...)` — a boundary input that all three surfaces will encounter from real users and automated callers.
findings.location: AC section — `rebar idea '<title>'`, `create_idea`, `rebar.idea(...)` entry points

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
